messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "stock-vs-flow-intensity-mismatch",
 "title": "tamBase apportions revenue by standing MW, but PDU revenue follows new-build flow — which is concentrating outside CE-7",
 "narrative": "The CE-7 = 39.8%-of-Europe hop divides standing colocation stock by standing stock. But rack-PDU revenue is fit-out-driven: it is earned when MW are delivered or refreshed, not while they sit installed. CBRE shows the flow concentrating away from CE: 57% of 2025's 937MW of new European supply lands in FLAPD (of which only Frankfurt and Amsterdam are CE-7), 85% of take-up is FLAPD, London alone exceeds 1GW and is Europe's largest market, and double-digit growth in secondary markets is going to Milan and Madrid — not Warsaw or Vienna. If CE-7's share of European capacity ADDITIONS is meaningfully below its 39.8% share of stock, the €300M systematically overstates where 2026 PDU purchase orders will actually be written, even if every input number is individually correct.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "tamBase"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "European colo revenue-generating activity is flow-shaped: 937MW of new supply in 2025, 57% of it in FLAPD, and 85% of take-up in FLAPD — a distribution measurably different from the standing-stock table the CE-7 ratio is computed from",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c6",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c7",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c9"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "London (>1GW, Europe's largest market) and Frankfurt together represent roughly half of European supply, and CBRE's tracked secondary-growth markets are Milan and Madrid — so the growth increment that a 9% CAGR is made of accrues disproportionately to non-CE-7 geographies, while CE-7's standing stock skews toward older, lower-density halls with slower PDU refresh",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c3",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c8",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c19"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The 39.8% stock ratio overstates CE-7's share of near-term PDU revenue; tamBase should be read as a stock-weighted ceiling, with the flow-weighted (order-relevant) base materially lower — a bias no band edge in the tornado captures because it is a method error, not parameter noise",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c4"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Stock apportionment is the standard top-down move and every input is sourced and recent — nothing on the page looks estimated. The rot is in an unstated physical assumption (PDU € per standing MW is uniform across regions and vintages) that no source states because no source measures it; you only see it by asking what event actually generates a PDU invoice.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "CBRE's 2025–26 delivery pipeline (or Statista's 2026-vintage country deltas) showing CE-7 capturing ≥38% of new European colocation MW delivered — i.e., flow share matching stock share.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.35,
  "rationale": "The FLAPD concentration of additions and take-up is well documented, but Frankfurt and Amsterdam are themselves FLAPD members inside CE-7, which claws back part of the flow; the net CE-7 flow deficit vs its stock share is real but plausibly 5–10 points rather than catastrophic",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "tamBase",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.8,
   "note": "Re-weight the CE-7 share from standing stock toward capacity-additions flow (~32% vs 39.8%)"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "CE-7 share of new European colocation MW delivered per quarter",
   "where": "CBRE 'Europe Data Centres Figures' quarterly PDFs (new supply by market)",
   "threshold": "CE-7 markets (Frankfurt, Amsterdam, Zurich, Warsaw, Vienna, Prague) <35% of tracked deliveries for two consecutive quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Growth differential between CE-7 and non-CE secondary markets",
   "where": "Statista Europe colocation power by country, 2026 vintage vs 2025; CBRE secondary-markets commentary (Milan, Madrid)",
   "threshold": "Non-CE secondary markets adding MW at ≥2x the CE-7 ex-DE/NL rate",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Rebuild the CE base on a two-component model: refresh revenue on standing stock plus fit-out revenue on the CBRE delivery pipeline, priced per MW from vendor BOMs",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "tamBase",
   "note": "Separates the stock-vs-flow question the current single ratio conflates"
  },
  {
   "action": "Position Year-1 offering around retrofit/refresh of CE-7's older standing stock (where stock-based sizing IS the right measure) rather than new-build tenders",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Aligns go-to-market with the component of the TAM the model measures correctly"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "definition-scopedown",
 "riskId": "risk.definition-scopedown.stock-vs-flow-intensity-mismatch"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

CBRE secondary-markets commentary (Milan, Madrid)","threshold":"Non-CE secondary markets adding MW at ≥2x the CE-7 ex-DE/NL rate","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Rebuild the CE base on a two-component model: refresh revenue on standing stock plus fit-out revenue on the CBRE delivery pipeline, priced per MW from vendor BOMs","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"Separates the stock-vs-flow question the current single ratio conflates"},{"action":"Position Year-1 offering around retrofit/refresh of CE-7's older standing stock (where stock-based sizing IS the right measure) rather than new-build tenders","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Aligns go-to-market with the component of the TAM the model measures correctly"}]}
